interaction_tables.note: Each interaction entry has a 'type' and a 'results' array (or 'function' for mechanic types). The code picks a random result from the array. Variables in {curly_braces} are replaced dynamically at runtime. Fallback: if a specific location override is not found, use the matching generic pool.

variable_reference.{location}: A random named location from the current settlement
variable_reference.{amount}: A gold amount appropriate to player tier (tier1: 5-25gp, tier2: 25-100gp, tier3: 100-500gp, tier4: 500+gp)
variable_reference.{creature}: A random creature name from the bestiary
variable_reference.{item}: A random item name from the item database
variable_reference.{spell}: A random spell name from the spell database
variable_reference.{district}: The name of the current district

interaction_types.table_result: Pick a random string from results array and add to journal as narrative log.
interaction_types.mechanic: Call an existing game function. 'function' field names the handler.
interaction_types.cost_action: Show a confirmation dialog with cost. On confirm, deduct gold and apply effect.
interaction_types.oracle_prompt: Pre-fill the Oracle input with the suggested question and open the Oracle panel.
interaction_types.skill_check: Trigger a skill check with the specified skill and DC. Use results.success and results.failure.

results: An old man nursing his drink leans over: 'They say something came out of {location} last night. Nobody's gone back to check.'
A merchant passing through mentions that the road to the east has been unusually quiet lately. Too quiet.
'You didn't hear this from me,' whispers a server, 'but {npc_name} the {npc_role} hasn't been seen in three days. Friends are getting worried.'
A group of laborers argue about whether the new construction near {location} is cursed. Two of them quit this morning.
Someone left a coded message pinned to the notice board. No one's claimed it.
'The guard doubled their patrols near {location} last night,' a local mentions. 'Won't say why.'
A traveler just arrived from the south. Whatever they saw on the road, they won't talk about it.
Word is that {npc_name} is looking for someone with specific skills. Paying well, no questions asked.
A neighbor mentions that the family at {location} packed up and left in the middle of the night last week.
'The children won't go near the old fountain anymore,' someone says. 'They say they see faces in the water.'
A street vendor complains that their best supplier has been unreliable lately. Something has disrupted trade from the south.
Two locals debate loudly about whether the new {npc_role} in the {district} district is trustworthy. The argument goes nowhere.
A young woman is asking about her missing brother. He worked at {location} and hasn't come home.
'Strange lights again last night,' an elderly man says, gesturing vaguely toward the edge of town.
The baker mentions that the flour delivery was late and the cart driver looked frightened.
Someone chalked a symbol on the wall near {location}. Nobody recognizes it. Nobody is asking questions.
A child runs past shouting about something at {location}. By the time you catch them, they've forgotten what the fuss was.
Two beggars argue over territory near the market. One of them seems to know more about what happens in this district than most guards.
'Keep your coin close,' a passerby warns you without making eye contact. 'Cutpurses working this block today.'
Graffiti near {location} reads something in a language you half-recognize. Could be a gang sign, could be a warning.
A street performer stops their act to stare at something behind you. When you turn, nothing is there. When you turn back, the performer is gone.
A cart wheel breaks in front of {location}, spilling its load. The owner is frantic and won't say what the crates contain.
'You're new here,' a child says, studying you. 'The last new person who asked too many questions left quickly.'
Someone has been watching you from across the street for the past few minutes. When you look directly at them, they move on.
An old record mentions that the district you're in was built over something older. The original plans were sealed by order of the governor.
'This city has had three names,' a historian tells you. 'The first two were abandoned for reasons nobody writes down.'
A faded inscription near {location} commemorates an event that official histories don't mention.
The archivist mentions offhandedly that entire sections of the city records from a particular decade are missing.
'Ask the old families,' someone suggests. 'They remember what the official story leaves out.'
A map on the wall of a local establishment shows the city as it was a hundred years ago. Several landmarks no longer exist. One of them should.
A local historian mentions that the founding of this settlement involved a bargain. They won't say with whom.
There's a sealed room in {location}. Been sealed for forty years. The current owner doesn't know why and is too superstitious to open it.
A contact speaks quietly: 'Someone is moving something large through the city. Whatever it is, the usual fences won't touch it.'
'The guild has a problem,' a source mentions. 'Someone on the inside has been talking to the guard. Nobody knows who yet.'
Three independent operators have gone silent in the past tenday. Could be coincidence. Probably isn't.
'There's a job nobody wants to take,' a fence mentions. 'The pay is exceptional. That should tell you something.'
Word is that a magistrate has been accepting payments for information. The question is who's paying.
A shipment was supposed to arrive at {location} last night. It didn't. The people waiting for it are very unhappy.
'Stay away from {location} this week,' a contact advises. 'I don't know why. Just stay away.'
Someone new is operating in the district. Efficient, quiet, no known affiliations. Everyone is nervous.
A minor official mentions that two factions on the council are deadlocked over something they won't discuss in open session.
'The governor has been meeting privately with someone from outside the city,' a clerk whispers. 'Arrived after dark, left before dawn.'
A vote is coming that will change how the district operates. Both sides are courting anyone with influence.
'Follow the building permits,' a cynical official suggests. 'You can read the political winds in where they allow construction.'
Three council members have changed their positions on a key issue in the past month. No one is explaining why.
An ambassador from a neighboring power arrived unexpectedly this week. The official reason is a courtesy visit. No one believes that.
A senior official was replaced quietly last tenday. Their replacement is unknown outside the inner circle.
'The city guard's loyalties are not as simple as they appear,' a contact in the government district observes.
The priest mentions that pilgrimages to a sacred site have increased dramatically this season. Something is drawing the faithful.
'There is a schism forming,' a devoted worshipper says quietly. 'Not here. But word travels.'
A holy artifact belonging to this faith was reportedly stolen three months ago. The temple has been quiet about it.
'Pray for the town of {location},' a priest asks you. 'They have need of it.' They won't say more.
A new interpretation of an old prophecy has been circulating among the clergy. Not everyone agrees it's benign.
The temple has been receiving unusual donations lately — not coin, but objects. Strange ones.
'Someone has been asking questions about our history,' the head priest says. 'Questions that suggest they know things they should not.'
A minor miracle was reported near {location} last week. The temple is investigating quietly.
A guard sergeant mentions that patrols near {location} have been increased after three incidents in the past week.
'Something is coming out of the ruins to the east at night,' a scout reports. 'We've lost two patrols trying to identify it.'
A bounty has been posted on a {creature} that has been attacking travelers on the northern road. Nobody's collected yet.
'The threat isn't outside the walls,' a veteran guard says darkly. 'It never was.'
A caravan was attacked two days from here. The survivors described something that didn't match any known creature.
Three separate farms reported the same thing on the same night. The guard is calling it coincidence. The farmers are not.
'We know what it is,' a guard tells you. 'We just don't know how to stop it.'
A map of recent incidents has been pinned in the barracks. There is a clear pattern in the locations. No one has noted it yet.
An apprentice mage reports that the mana flows near {location} have become unstable without any clear cause.
'The stars aren't where they should be,' an astrologer mutters. 'Someone is moving them.'
A confiscated grimoire mentions an incomplete ritual that might explain the recent distortions, but no one has managed to decipher it.
'This isn't new magic,' an elderly arcanist says. 'It's ancient magic waking up.'
Three spellcasters suffered the same kind of mental collapse after attempting to perceive what lies beyond the veil.
A merchant claims that some enchanted items are losing power, while others — inexplicably — are gaining it.
'We mapped the anomalies,' a researcher explains. 'They form a symbol. Not one you want to see.'
A shard of arcane crystal found near {location} vibrates when brought close to any source of magic.
A notice on the board: '{npc_name} seeks an experienced individual for a retrieval job. Discretion required. Pay: {amount} gp.'
A merchant approaches you quietly: 'I need someone to escort a package to {location}. No questions, good pay.'
A job: clear out whatever has taken up residence in an abandoned building near {location}. Owner wants it done quietly.
A local official needs documents delivered to three locations in the city by nightfall. {amount} gp for the job.
'You look capable,' a well-dressed person says. 'I need a capable person. Tonight. {amount} gp.'
A message has been left for anyone willing to investigate disappearances near {location}. No official involvement.
Work available: guard duty for a merchant's warehouse for three nights. {amount} gp per night.
A notice requests someone to find a missing person — last seen near {location} four days ago. Family is paying {amount} gp.
A mercenary captain is recruiting for a job outside the city. Won't say what, but the pay is {amount} gp and equipment is provided.
'Caravan needs swords,' a handler says bluntly. 'Three days south, three days back. {amount} gp, no sharing, no questions.'
A local lord needs a small group to clear a location that can't be identified officially. Very good pay. Very unofficial.
Bounty work: a dangerous individual was last seen near {location}. Wanted alive. {amount} gp.
A note pinned to the board: 'Soldiers of fortune wanted for a dangerous operation. Experience with {creature} preferred.'
'The city guard won't handle it,' a civilian explains. 'Too political. I need someone who doesn't care about politics.'
A job nobody else will take is still available. The pay reflects that: {amount} gp up front, more on completion.
A guild is hiring outside contractors for something they can't assign to their own members. Deniability is the point.
The board has three new notices today. One of them catches your eye: someone needs a package retrieved from {location}. {amount} gp.
A notice in a careful hand: 'Seeking individual with knowledge of {creature}. Academic interest. Generous compensation.'
Someone has crossed out and rewritten a notice multiple times. The job is still available. Whatever it involves, people keep declining.
A notice from the city guard: reward offered for information about recent incidents near {location}. No questions asked.
You post your own notice. Within the hour, someone has already added a comment beneath it.
Three notices are from the same person, each offering more than the last. Whatever they need done, they need it done soon.
A notice in coded language. You're not sure what it's offering, but someone has circled it in charcoal.
An official notice warns against a wanted individual. The description is vague enough to match several people you've met.

Long rest.confirmation: Take a long rest here? Your HP and abilities will be restored.

Rest safely.confirmation: Rest here safely through the night?

results: '{npc_name} the {npc_role} was seen leaving {location} in a hurry yesterday evening. Nobody knows why.'
'I shouldn't say this, but the {npc_role} at the end of the street has been acting strange since last week.'
'They say the new {npc_role} in the {district} district isn't who they claim to be. But who's going to ask?'
'Something happened at {location} last month. The people involved won't talk about it. That's usually a bad sign.'
'Did you hear? {npc_name} lost everything at the gambling house. Three nights in a row. Some say it wasn't bad luck.'
'The family that used to live here moved out suddenly. Left most of their things. Nobody's renting the place.'
'A stranger was asking questions about the old families last tenday. Haven't seen them since.'
'There's a meeting tonight at {location}. Private. Very private. Word travels though.'
A tense exchange between two merchants near {location} ends abruptly when they notice you watching.
Children play a game in the street with rules you don't recognize. When you ask, they look at each other and run.
A cart unloads crates at {location} while the owner watches nervously, counting each one.
Two guards on patrol walk past without acknowledging each other. The tension between them is obvious.
A street performer draws a crowd, but their act seems to be a cover for something. Hard to say what.
Someone is sketching the street from a window above. They close the shutter when they notice you looking up.
An argument spills briefly into the street from a building near {location}, then stops suddenly.
A well-dressed individual walks through the district looking at everything and touching nothing, taking notes.
A {npc_role} named {npc_name} introduces themselves quietly. They seem to know more about you than they should. Could be useful.
Someone slides a note to you. It has a name, a location, and a time. Nothing else.
A regular here — {npc_name} — takes an interest in you. Not a friendly interest. A professional one.
You catch someone's eye across the room. A moment of mutual assessment. They nod slightly. A beginning.
'{npc_name} can help you with that,' a stranger says, unprompted. They leave before you can ask how they knew what you needed.
A {npc_role} buys you a drink without explanation. They want something. They'll mention it when the time is right.
You're approached by someone who asks three very specific questions about your skills. Then they give you a name and walk away.
A contact from a previous encounter passes you a name: '{npc_name}. {district} district. Tell them I sent you.'
A fence named {npc_name} takes note of you. They work out of {location}. They'll deal with the right kind of person.
Someone slides a chit across the table. It's an introduction token — a way to open a door that's usually closed.
A {npc_role} speaks to you briefly in what seems like small talk. It isn't. By the end you have a name and a signal.
You're tested first — asked a question with no right answer. The fact that you don't panic seems to be the answer.
'{npc_name} owes me,' a contact says. 'Now they owe you. Use it carefully.'
A note is passed to you: a time, a location, no name. Someone wants to meet.
A regular here is clearly connected. They don't approach you, but they haven't looked away either.
The introduction is indirect — a name passed through two intermediaries. Someone doesn't want to be seen talking to you directly. Yet.
The healer's hands glow faintly as they work. Your wounds close and the pain recedes.
A brief prayer, a touch of light. You feel your injuries knit themselves closed.
The priest administers treatment with practiced efficiency. You leave feeling considerably better.
It's not magic exactly — more like guided natural recovery. Effective, regardless.
The healer frowns at your injuries, then at you. 'What did you do?' They treat you anyway.
A novice acolyte tends to your wounds carefully. Not a full recovery, but enough.
The shrine keeper says a brief blessing over you. The worst of your injuries ease.
Simple treatment — cleaned wounds, a binding, a minor prayer. It helps.
The healer has limited supplies but does what they can. You feel somewhat better.
The clerk weighs your coins carefully and quotes a fair rate. Standard transaction, no complications.
The exchange rate today is slightly unfavorable. The clerk shrugs. 'Market conditions.'
A quick, professional transaction. The clerk doesn't ask where the coins came from.
The clerk notices a marking on one of your coins and examines it carefully before completing the exchange.
Exchange completed. The clerk adds: 'If you're carrying that much regularly, we offer secure storage.'
A case is being heard involving a property dispute near {location}. The arguments reveal more about local politics than property law.
A minor criminal matter. The sentence seems disproportionate to the offense. No one in the room looks surprised.
The session is interrupted by an objection that clearly wasn't expected. The official presiding looks uncomfortable.
An unusually large number of observers today. Whatever is being decided here matters to people.
The session ends abruptly. An official whispers something to the presiding figure and the room is cleared.
A routine proceeding becomes interesting when a witness says something they clearly weren't supposed to say.
The gallery is full of people from the {district} district. They watch the proceedings with focused intensity.
An empty chair at the official table. No explanation given. Several observers exchange meaningful looks.

Buy food and drink.confirmation: Buy a day's worth of food and drink for 1 gp?
results: The food is simple but filling. You eat well enough.
Better than expected for the price. You make a note of this place.
Nothing special, but it does the job. You leave satisfied.
The server recommends a local specialty. It's worth it.
The artist studies you carefully. 'Yes… I can capture this. Return in 1d4 days.'
'A portrait?' the artist says, intrigued. 'I will need special pigments. Bring me a gemstone worth at least {amount}.'
The atelier has a few pigments and brushes left over from recent work. Prices vary depending on quality.
The artist offers you a unique pigment made from crushed minerals. 'Rare stuff. Worth every coin.'
'Supplies? I'm nearly out,' the artist says. 'Try the {location} — they receive shipments more often.'

results.success: Your instructor praises your progress. You gain 1 day of effective training toward instrument proficiency.
results.failure: The lesson is frustrating and unfocused. No progress is made today.

results: A talented student agrees to perform for you. Their repertoire includes traditional songs and local ballads. They ask you for 1 gp
The school's maestro is available — for a higher fee. Their performance is said to move even the sternest hearts. They ask you for 10 gp
No one is available today. 'Try again tomorrow,' the assistant suggests.
You watch a stirring performance. The crowd erupts in applause as the final act concludes. The show costs 5 gp
The play is mediocre, but the atmosphere is lively. You overhear whispers about {npc_name} backstage.
A last-minute cancellation forces the troupe to improvise. The result is chaotic but memorable.
A charismatic actor agrees to assist you — for the right price. They specialize in impersonations.  They ask you for 10 gp
A stagehand suggests a performer, {npc_name}, known for dramatic flair. 'They love adventure more than coin. You can find them in {location}'
No actors are available. The troupe is preparing for an important premiere.
The director eyes you critically. 'We need someone for a minor role. Interested?'
A stagehand offers you a job moving props. It's not glamorous, but it pays {amount}.
'Work?' the manager laughs. 'Try the {location}. They always need extra hands.'
You test the strings of a finely crafted lute. Its resonance is warm and bright — the shopkeeper watches your reaction with a knowing smile.
A peculiar horn catches your eye. The vendor taps it lightly and it emits a soft, haunting note that lingers in the air.
You find a dusty instrument tucked behind a shelf. 'Old piece,' the vendor says. 'Belonged to {npc_name} a {npc_role}, if the rumors are true.'
A row of exotic instruments from distant lands gleams under lantern light. One of them vibrates faintly as you approach.
The vendor proudly displays a newly arrived masterpiece. 'Only one like it in the region,' they say. 'Try it — gently.'
The vendor inspects your instrument with professional care. 'Good craftsmanship. I can offer {amount}.'
'Where did you get this?' the vendor asks, intrigued. They run a finger along the wood grain, clearly impressed.
The vendor shakes their head. 'Too many of these in stock. Try again next week — the market shifts quickly.'
A student overhears the conversation and immediately expresses interest. The vendor mediates a quick sale.
The vendor offers a lower price than expected, citing minor wear. Their explanation is annoyingly reasonable.
'Performers?' the vendor says. 'Try the {location}. A troupe arrived yesterday — loud bunch.'
The vendor mentions a traveling bard known for stirring ballads. 'They stay near the {location} when they're in town.'
'If you're looking for talent, speak with {npc_name}. They recruit musicians for private events.'
The vendor lowers their voice. 'There's a performer who plays only at secret gatherings. Ask around the {district}.'
'Funny you ask,' the vendor says. 'Someone was looking for a musician earlier today. Left no name.'
The vendor nods eagerly. 'I can craft that. Give me 1d6 days and I'll have something worthy of your hands. It will cost you 30 gp.'
'Custom work?' they say, rubbing their chin. 'I'll need rare materials. Bring me a {item} and we can begin.'
The vendor refuses politely. 'Too complex for my workshop. But {npc_name} might take the commission. You should go to {location}'
They sketch a quick design on parchment. 'Yes… this could work. But it will cost more than you expect becuase there is a shortage of materials nowdays. They ask for 45 gp'
The vendor agrees, but warns: 'If you want perfection, you'll need to be patient. Art can't be rushed. Give me 10 days and 50 gp and you will have a masterpiece.'
A painting of a stormy coastline draws your attention. The waves seem almost alive under the gallery's soft lighting.
You pause before a sculpture carved from translucent stone. It shifts color subtly as you move around it.
A curator approaches quietly. 'That piece,' they whisper, 'was recovered from {location}. Its history is… complicated.'
A series of portraits lines the wall — each subject's eyes seem to follow you with unsettling precision.
You find a small, unassuming sketch tucked between grander works. The curator smiles. 'That one is my favorite.'
The curator examines your item with reverence. 'Exquisite. I can offer {amount}, assuming the provenance checks out.'
'Where did you acquire this?' the curator asks sharply. Their tone softens once you explain.
The gallery declines politely. 'We specialize in a different style. Try the {location} instead.'
A wealthy patron overhears and expresses interest. Negotiations begin immediately.
The curator offers a surprisingly high price. 'Pieces like this rarely surface. I'd be a fool to refuse.'

results.success: The curator provides a detailed appraisal. 'This piece is authentic — and valuable. Handle it with care.'
results.failure: The curator frowns. 'Difficult to say. I need more time, or perhaps access to the archives at {location}.'

results: 'Provenance?' the curator repeats. 'Ah, a fascinating tale. It begins in the {district}, decades ago…'
They produce a dusty ledger. 'Your object appears in these records — linked to {npc_name}, a collector of some renown.'
'I've heard rumors,' the curator says. 'Some claim the piece was stolen from a noble house. Others say it was gifted.'
The curator hesitates. 'Its origins are… disputed. Several factions have shown interest in it recently.'
'This object,' they say quietly, 'was once part of a larger set. The rest may still be hidden in {location}.'
A charm shaped like a coiled serpent hums faintly as you lift it. The runes along its spine glow for a heartbeat.
You examine a small wooden talisman etched with swirling sigils of the spell {spell}. The shopkeeper warns: 'It reacts to strong emotions.'
A shimmering pendant catches your eye. 'Found near {location},' the vendor says. 'Strange place. Stranger energies.'
A set of rune-etched stones sits in a velvet-lined box. When you touch one, it vibrates softly, as if greeting you.
You notice a charm wrapped in silver thread. 'Don't touch that one,' the vendor says quickly. 'It's… temperamental.'

results.success: The tattooist nods approvingly. 'You understand the runes well enough. I can inscribe a minor protective sigil. Return in 1d4 days.'
results.failure: The tattooist frowns. 'These runes are not toys. Study more, then return — I won't risk a misbinding.'

results: An apprentice mage reports that the mana flows near {location} have become unstable without any clear cause.
'The stars aren't where they should be,' an astrologer mutters. 'Someone is moving them.'
A confiscated grimoire mentions an incomplete ritual that might explain the recent distortions, but no one has managed to decipher it.
'This isn't new magic,' an elderly arcanist says. 'It's ancient magic waking up.'
Three spellcasters suffered the same kind of mental collapse after attempting to perceive what lies beyond the veil.
A merchant claims that some enchanted items are losing power, while others — inexplicably — are gaining it.
'We mapped the anomalies,' a researcher explains. 'They form a symbol. Not one you want to see.'
A shard of arcane crystal found near {location} vibrates when brought close to any source of magic.
A troupe of dancers spins in perfect rhythm. The crowd claps along, and a child hands you a flower with a shy smile.
A lone bard plays a haunting melody. As the final note fades, someone whispers a rumor about {npc_name}.
A fire-breather draws gasps from the crowd. Sparks drift upward, briefly forming shapes that resemble runes.
A juggler drops a ball, laughs it off, and improvises a comedic routine that has the crowd roaring.
A masked performer recites a dramatic monologue. Their eyes meet yours for a moment — unsettlingly intense.

results.success: Your performance draws a small crowd. You earn {amount} and a few appreciative nods.
results.failure: Your act fails to impress. A child claps politely, but no one else seems moved.

results: A hooded figure approaches quietly. 'You look like someone who listens. Meet me at {location} after dusk.'
A performer introduces you to {npc_name}, who claims to know things about the {district}.
You overhear a whispered argument between two individuals. One of them drops a folded note as they leave.
A merchant compliments your taste and hints at a private gathering happening soon — invitation only.
A street performer recognizes you. 'I've heard your name before,' they say. 'Someone's been asking about you.'

The vendor's eyes sharpen with interest. 'Now that is a question. I had a scroll of {spell} pass through last month — sold it before I could copy it. Regret that.' They pause. 'Try the Wizard's Tower. Or {npc_name} — private collector, very private.'
'Rare how?' the vendor asks. 'Rare as in difficult, or rare as in shouldn't exist?' Either way, they suggest checking back in a tenday — a shipment is expected.
The vendor produces a worn ledger. '{spell} — had one, sold one, haven't seen another. The person who bought it came from the academy. That's all I know.'
'I know someone who knows someone,' the vendor says carefully. 'This kind of thing has a price beyond coin. Are you serious about this?'
The vendor shakes their head slowly. 'Whoever told you to come here for that overestimates my stock. But I can send word if something surfaces. Name?'
'An {npc_role} named {npc_name} comes here every ten days to buy {spell} scrolls. I've never understood why they need them.'

results.success: The vendor agrees to copy the spell onto a scroll for you. Cost: 25 gp per spell level. Available in 1d4 days.
results.failure: The vendor declines. 'I don't copy spells I don't have. And I don't copy spells I have for just anyone.' They suggest returning with a better reason.

results: The vendor examines your scroll carefully, holds it up to the light, and names a price. Fair enough.
'Where did you get this?' The question is professional, not accusatory. The price they offer reflects the answer.
The vendor declines to purchase. 'I have three of these already. Come back when the market shifts.'
The vendor makes a counteroffer lower than you expected. They explain why, and they're not wrong.
A quick sale. The vendor seems more interested in the scroll than the transaction. They're already reading it before you leave.
The current stock includes several items under glass. The vendor watches you as you browse — not suspiciously, professionally.
An item near the back catches your eye. The vendor notices immediately. 'Good eye. That one isn't officially for sale yet.'
The stock is well-curated if limited. Whatever they carry, they know exactly what it does.
A new item arrived this morning — the vendor is still cataloguing it. They'll discuss it with serious buyers only.
Two items in the display case have been there a while. The vendor doesn't push them. They're waiting for the right buyer.

results.success: The vendor examines your item carefully. After several minutes: 'This is a {item}. My assessment: genuine, functional, worth approximately {amount} gp on the current market.' They charge 10 gp for the appraisal.
results.failure: The vendor glances at your item and shakes their head. 'I don't appraise items I didn't sell. Too much liability.' They suggest the Wizard's Tower.

results: 'I don't have one now,' the vendor says, 'but I know where one is. Finding it would cost you {amount} gp just for the introduction.'
The vendor makes a note. 'I'll put the word out. If something surfaces, I'll send you a message. Where are you staying?'
'That item is controlled,' the vendor says carefully. 'Not illegal to own. Complicated to acquire. Interested in complicated?'
'I had one six months ago. Sold it the day it arrived.' A pause. 'The person who bought it is still in the city, as far as I know.'
The vendor seems unsurprised by the request. 'You're not the first to ask about that. Something is driving demand. I don't know what yet.'
The vendor examines the item thoroughly before naming a price. Their offer is fair — 60% of market value.
'I'll take it,' the vendor says after a careful inspection, 'but I need provenance. Where did this come from?'
The vendor declines politely. 'I have two already. The market for this is slow right now.'
An offer is made immediately, no examination. That speed suggests they know exactly what it is and have a buyer waiting.
The vendor haggles. Not aggressively, but they know the item's value and won't pretend otherwise.
The wizard listens, asks three clarifying questions, and names a price: {amount} gp, two weeks minimum. 'Magic research isn't predictable. It might take longer.'
'I can investigate this,' the wizard says slowly, 'but I'll need something in return beyond coin. There is a matter I need assistance with.'
The wizard declines the commission but suggests a colleague who specializes in the relevant school. Provides a name and location.
'That research has already been done,' the wizard says, pulling a scroll from a shelf. 'By me, twelve years ago. The results were not encouraging.'
The wizard agrees, but sets a condition: whatever is discovered is shared equally. 'Knowledge shouldn't be hoarded. Especially not dangerous knowledge.'

Request identification of a magic item.confirmation: Pay 100 gp for a full magical identification?
results: The wizard examines the item for several minutes in silence. 'This is more interesting than I expected.' They provide a full accounting of its properties.
A ritual identification — takes an hour, costs {amount} gp, reveals everything the item can do and a fragment of its history.
'Interesting,' the wizard murmurs, turning the item over. They identify its properties and add: 'I would be curious to know where you found this.'
The identification reveals something the wizard didn't expect. They charge the standard fee but ask to study it further — for their own reasons.
The wizard sets down their work and gives the question genuine consideration. 'That is not a simple matter. The short answer is yes. The accurate answer requires context.'
'I wrote a monograph on that subject,' the wizard says, moving to a shelf. 'Forty pages. I can summarize the relevant sections for {amount} gp.'
The wizard's expression shifts slightly — not alarm, but attention. 'Why are you asking about that specifically?'
'The conventional understanding is incomplete,' the wizard says. 'Most practitioners don't know what I know about this. That is intentional.'
An unexpected answer: the wizard doesn't know. They admit it without embarrassment. 'But I know who does. Whether they'll tell you is another matter.'
A student assistant hands you a neatly organized kit. 'Standard issue,' they say. 'Reliable, if not glamorous.'
You find a shelf of discounted components. 'Overstock from last semester,' the clerk explains.
A shimmering focus crystal catches your eye. 'Careful,' the assistant warns. 'It resonates with strong emotions.'
A professor interrupts your browsing. 'If you're buying that,' they say, pointing at a reagent, 'use it responsibly.'
A crate is being unpacked nearby. 'Fresh shipment from {location},' the clerk says. 'Good quality this time.'

results.success: A professor approves your application. 'Classes begin tomorrow. Bring your own materials.'

results: The librarian grants you access to the lower stacks. 'No spell copying without permission,' they warn.
You find a dusty tome referencing {spell}. Several pages are missing — intentionally.
A student whispers: 'If you're looking for rare texts, check the restricted shelves… if you can get permission.'
A professor catches you browsing. 'Researching something specific?' they ask, eyeing your notes.
You discover a marginal note in an old book. It mentions {npc_name} and a forgotten ritual.
You pin your notice to the board. Within minutes, a curious student asks for details.
A professor glances at your posting and nods approvingly. 'Ambitious,' they say.
A rival posting nearby catches your eye — someone else is seeking help with a similar task.
A student removes your notice, reads it, and replaces it with a scribbled question: 'Is this dangerous?'
A clerk warns you: 'Jobs involving planar magic require approval from the dean.'
A shelf of glittering components hums faintly as you pass. The vendor watches to see which one reacts to you.
You find a focus carved from bone. 'Ethically sourced,' the vendor assures you before you can ask.
A jar of powdered gemstone pulses with inner light. 'Careful,' the vendor says. 'It stains… permanently.'
A rack of wands stands neatly arranged. One of them twitches slightly when you reach for it.
A crate labeled 'fragile' sits unopened. The vendor mutters: 'Shipment from {location}. Always unpredictable.'
The vendor inspects your components with a jeweler's lens. 'Good quality. I can offer {amount}.'
'Where did you get this?' the vendor asks, intrigued. They weigh the component carefully in their palm.
The vendor declines politely. 'Too unstable for resale. Try the academy — they take risks.'
A customer overhears and immediately expresses interest. The vendor mediates a quick negotiation.
The vendor offers a lower price than expected, citing market saturation. Their reasoning is annoyingly sound.
'Unusual?' the vendor repeats. 'Try the {location}. They deal in… stranger things.'
The vendor lowers their voice. 'Someone came asking for the same thing yesterday. Odd coincidence.'
'I had a rare reagent,' the vendor says. 'Sold it to {npc_name}. They seemed nervous.'
The vendor pulls out a locked box. 'I do have something… but it's not for sale to just anyone.'
'Unusual components attract unusual attention,' the vendor warns. 'Be sure you know what you're doing.'
The vendor mentions strange lights seen above the {district} at night.

A customer interrupts: 'Did you hear? {npc_name} vanished after experimenting with a new spell.'
The vendor leans in. 'There's talk of a magical disturbance beneath the city. Old, powerful, and waking.'
'Rumors?' the vendor says. 'Plenty. But most of them are dangerous to repeat.'

results.success: The alchemist considers your request. 'Possible. Unusual, but possible. Cost: {amount} gp, five days. No guarantees on potency until I test a batch.'
results.failure: The alchemist shakes their head. 'I don't brew custom orders for people I don't know. Come back with a recommendation from a regular customer.'

results.success: The alchemist lowers their voice. 'Academically speaking — and I mean academically — what you're describing sounds like {creature} venom. The antidote isn't kept on shelves. But I can have it ready in six hours if you need it.'
results.failure: 'I sell reagents and potions,' the alchemist says flatly. 'I don't consult on poisons. Try somewhere less reputable.'

results: The alchemist examines your materials with professional interest. 'Decent quality. I'll offer {amount} gp for the lot.'
'Where did you find this?' The question is genuine curiosity, not suspicion. The offer reflects the rarity.
'I have more of this than I need right now,' the alchemist says apologetically. 'Half price, or I can hold it and pay full when my stock depletes.'
A quick transaction — the alchemist clearly needed exactly what you brought. The price is better than expected.
A merchant waves you over enthusiastically. 'Fresh stock from {location}! Best prices you'll find today.'
You spot a stall selling curious trinkets. One of them vibrates faintly when you touch it.
A vendor offers you a sample of dried fruit. 'First taste is free,' they say with a wink.
A heavily laden cart arrives, and the merchant begins unpacking crates stamped with unfamiliar sigils.
You find a rack of tools and gear. A merchant leans in: 'That one? Reliable. Saved my life once.'
A merchant inspects your goods with a practiced eye. 'I can offer {amount}, no more.'
'Where did you get this?' a vendor asks, intrigued. Their offer rises slightly after your explanation.
A merchant declines politely. 'Too niche for my customers. Try the {location} instead.'
A bidding war erupts between two merchants, each trying to outdo the other.
A vendor offers a low price, but hints that {npc_name} might pay more for such an item.
You locate the merchant quickly — their stall is loud, colorful, and impossible to miss.
After searching for a while, you find the merchant packing up. 'You're lucky,' they say. 'I was just leaving.'
You ask around, but no one seems to know where the merchant is today.
A vendor points you toward a distant stall. 'They moved locations recently. Follow the smell of spices.'
You find someone claiming to be the merchant's assistant. Their information is… questionable.
You pin your notice to a crowded board. Within minutes, someone tears off a tab with your contact mark.
A merchant reads your notice and nods approvingly. 'Ambitious,' they say.
A child runs up and asks what your notice means. Their curiosity draws a small crowd.
Someone has posted a similar notice beside yours — almost too similar.
A vendor warns you: 'Posting that might attract the wrong kind of attention.'
The shopkeeper hands you a sturdy piece of gear. 'Reliable. Tested it myself in the field.'
A rack of weapons gleams under lantern light. One blade hums faintly when you lift it.
You find a set of armor with dents and scratches. 'Battle-tested,' the shopkeeper says proudly.
A crate of new stock arrives. The shopkeeper mutters: 'Finally. Been waiting on this shipment for weeks.'
A curious item catches your eye. 'Not standard issue,' the shopkeeper admits. 'But it works.'
The shopkeeper inspects your salvage with a veteran's eye. 'Good haul. I can offer {amount}.'
'Where did you find this?' they ask, impressed. Their offer increases slightly.
The shopkeeper declines. 'Too damaged. Not worth the repair cost.'
A customer overhears and immediately expresses interest. The shopkeeper mediates a quick deal.
The shopkeeper offers a lower price than expected, citing market saturation. Their reasoning is solid.
'Dungeons?' the shopkeeper says. 'Try the outskirts near {location}. Something's been stirring there.'
They mention a cave system rumored to house a dangerous {creature}.
'Someone came in earlier asking the same thing,' the shopkeeper says. 'Looked nervous.'
The shopkeeper leans in. 'There's talk of an old ruin beneath the {district}. Not many return from it.'
'Threats? Plenty,' they say. 'But the worst ones don't advertise themselves.'
You find a posting seeking escorts for a caravan leaving tomorrow.
A notice catches your eye: someone is paying well for a rare {item}.
A job posting has been hastily crossed out. The ink is still wet.
You post your own notice. A grizzled adventurer reads it and nods thoughtfully.
A clerk warns you: 'Jobs involving the {district} require extra caution.'

results.success: After several hours of searching, you find relevant records. The information reveals: use Oracle to determine what was found. The archivist notes this is the second inquiry about this subject this month.
results.failure: The records are incomplete, misfiled, or deliberately obscured. You find fragments — enough to confirm the subject exists in the historical record, not enough to draw conclusions. A more thorough search would require a week.

results: The property records for {location} reveal three ownership changes in the past decade. The most recent transfer was unusual — no listed price, no listed buyer.
The archivist notes that this is the second request on this topic this month. The other came from an {npc_role} named {npc_name}.
The archivist retrieves the records and seems surprised by something in them. They don't say what. They answer your questions carefully.
The property records for this district were partially destroyed in a fire fifteen years ago. The gaps are... suggestive.
Standard records. Nothing unusual. Except that a clerk comes to watch you read after about twenty minutes.
The records show that {npc_name}, described as {npc_role}, purchased three properties in {district} on the same day.

Pay for expedited research.confirmation: Pay 25 gp for expedited research by the archive staff?
results: The archivist assigns a junior researcher to your request. Results within a few hours. What they find, use the Oracle to determine.
Expedited research completed. The lead researcher adds a personal note: 'You should be aware that someone else requested access to these same records recently.'
Results delivered. Thorough, professional, and accompanied by a quiet warning: 'Whoever you're looking into has powerful friends. That's not a threat. It's a fact.'

results.success: The steward studies your request, nods, and gestures you inside. 'The governor will see you shortly. Mind your manners.'
results.failure: The steward shakes their head. 'The governor is unavailable. Return with proper documentation or a stronger recommendation.'

results: A clerk accepts your notice and stamps it with an official seal. 'It will be posted within the hour.'
You pin your notice to a crowded board. A guard eyes it, then you, with mild suspicion.
A bureaucrat reviews your notice. 'This wording is vague. It may be challenged.'
A noble passing by glances at your notice and frowns. 'Bold claim,' they mutter.
A clerk quietly warns you: 'Posting such things can attract unwanted attention.'
A clerk hands you a stack of forms. 'Fill these out. All of them.'
The steward reviews your request. 'This requires approval from {npc_name}. They're difficult to reach.'
A guard directs you to a side office. 'They handle special permissions there.'
A bureaucrat sighs. 'Another writ? Very well. Expect delays.'
You receive a provisional permit. 'Temporary,' the clerk warns. 'Don't push your luck.'
The chamber is filled with heated debate. A council member slams their fist on the table.
A quiet session today — mostly procedural matters. Still, you catch a mention of {location}.
A citizen interrupts the council with a passionate plea. The room erupts in murmurs.
A council member delivers a speech hinting at unrest in the {district}.
A clerk hands out agendas. One item is mysteriously redacted.

results.success: The council member listens intently. 'You make a compelling case. I'll bring this to the next session.'
results.failure: The council member dismisses you with a polite smile. 'I have more pressing matters today.'

results: A clerk records your claim and stamps it with an official seal. 'Expect a response within a tenday.'
Your claim is accepted, but the clerk warns: 'This may provoke {npc_name}. Proceed carefully.'
A bureaucrat scrutinizes your paperwork. 'Incomplete. You'll need additional witnesses.'
A council aide quietly suggests an alternative route: 'Try the Governor's Palace. Faster results.'
Your claim is filed, but you overhear two clerks whispering about its potential consequences.

Long rest.confirmation: Pay 5 gp for the long rest?
results: You rest in a modest room. The muffled sounds of laughter and clinking mugs drift up from below.
A warm bed and a quiet corner offer you a peaceful night. You wake to the smell of fresh bread.
The innkeeper checks on you once during the night. 'Just making sure you're comfortable,' they say.
A bard plays softly downstairs as you drift to sleep. Their melody lingers in your dreams.
You awaken refreshed, sunlight filtering through thin curtains and dust motes dancing in the air.

Buy food and drink.confirmation: Pay 25 cp for the long rest?
results: The innkeeper serves a hearty stew. 'Family recipe,' they say proudly.
A mug of frothy ale is placed before you. 'Brewed locally — strong stuff.'
You try a plate of roasted meat seasoned with herbs from {location}. The flavor is rich and earthy.
A traveler shares their table and offers you a taste of their exotic drink. 'Acquired taste,' they warn.
The cook brings out a fresh loaf of bread. 'Still warm,' they say with a grin.
'Someone saw strange lights near {location} last night,' a patron whispers.
A drunkard mutters about {npc_name} being involved in something dangerous.
'There's trouble brewing in the {district},' the innkeeper says quietly.
A traveler claims they barely escaped a {creature} on the road.
'Rumors?' a bard laughs. 'I've got plenty — but they all come with a price.'
You find a notice seeking escorts for a caravan leaving at dawn.
A posting offers good coin for retrieving a lost {item}.
Someone has scribbled over a job notice, replacing details with cryptic symbols.
You post your own notice. A hooded figure reads it and nods slowly.
A barmaid warns you: 'Jobs involving the {district} tend to end badly.'

results.success: You play the table with subtle bluffs and careful reads. The pot grows steadily in your favor, and you walk away with {amount} gp — and a few suspicious glances.
results.failure: Your tells are obvious tonight. A sharp-eyed player calls your bluff, and you lose your stake. As you stand, someone mutters, 'Better luck next time… if you dare return.'

results: A contract is offered: retrieve a specific item from {location}. The owner won't be home. {amount} gp on delivery, no questions about condition.
The work available tonight: surveillance on {npc_name}. Watch, record, report. {amount} gp. Don't be seen.
A message needs to be delivered to someone who doesn't want to be found. {amount} gp. You'll know them by the mark they wear.
'Someone needs discouraging,' the handler says flatly. 'Not harmed. Discouraged. {amount} gp. You decide how.'
The contract is simple: find out what's being stored at {location} and report back. No removal, no confrontation. {amount} gp.

results.success: The fence examines your goods without comment and offers {amount} gp — roughly 40% of market value. Standard rates for standard risk.
results.failure: The fence shakes their head. 'Too hot. I know where this came from and I'm not touching it for at least a month. Come back then.'

results: A contact pulls you aside: 'Someone has been paying for information about you specifically. Started three days ago. I don't know who.'
'The job at {location} went wrong,' someone mentions quietly. 'Wrong enough that the people who planned it are lying low. If you were connected to it, now is the time to not be.'
'New player in the district,' a source confirms. 'Professional, well-funded, not affiliated with any existing operation. Everyone's watching.'
Useful intelligence: a guard patrol has been rerouted, creating a window of opportunity near {location}. Window closes in two days.
'The guild has a leak,' your contact says. 'I'm telling you because I trust you. That's rarer than you think.'

results.success: A brief conversation with the right signals exchanged. Access granted. The stock here includes things that aren't available in legitimate shops — at prices that reflect the risk.
results.failure: You're turned away. Either your approach was wrong, your credentials insufficient, or someone decided you weren't worth the risk today.

results: Work available: move a package from one location to another without inspection. Don't open it. {amount} gp.
'Someone needs to disappear from the city,' a contact says. 'Not killed — relocated. Quietly. {amount} gp if they're gone by morning.'
A distraction job: cause a commotion near {location} at a specific time. Loud enough to draw the guard. {amount} gp.
Acquisition work: there's a document in the City Archive that shouldn't exist. Remove it. {amount} gp, no records of this conversation.
'Straightforward work,' a handler says. 'Guard a shipment through the city tonight. You don't ask what's in it. {amount} gp.'

results.success: Sale completed. The buyer doesn't care about provenance. The price is low — 35% of market value — but the transaction is invisible.
results.failure: The buyer isn't interested or doesn't believe your story. 'Come back with something I can actually move. This is too recognizable right now.'

results.success: A good session at the tables. You walk away with more than you came with — {amount} gp profit. You also notice something about one of the other players worth remembering.
results.failure: A bad run. You lose {amount} gp. The house always has the edge, and tonight it showed. You do, however, overhear something interesting.

results: A regular at the tables mentions that someone has been losing large amounts deliberately — like they're paying for something through the house.
A dealer who owes you a favor leans over during a slow hand: '{npc_name} has been in here every night this week. Whatever they're worried about, they're not handling it well.'
'Someone was asking about you,' a card player says without looking up. 'Described you well. I didn't confirm anything.'
The house has a new backer. Nobody knows who. The old management is nervous. That nervousness is contagious.
A high-stakes game is being arranged for tomorrow night. The players are not the usual crowd. The stakes suggest something beyond money is changing hands.
The dealer hands you a polished set of dice. 'Weighted? No — but they feel lucky.'
You examine a beautifully carved deck of cards. 'Imported from {location},' the vendor says.
A set of bone dice catches your eye. They feel unnaturally cold.
A gambler offers to sell you their personal set. 'Never failed me,' they claim — suspiciously.
You find a simple but reliable gaming set. 'Good for beginners,' the dealer says.

A gambler slips you a folded note. Inside is a name and a meeting place.
A dealer nods toward a back room. 'Someone wants to speak with you.'
A stranger offers you a drink and a dangerous proposition.
'If you're looking for work,' a quiet voice says, 'meet me near {location} after midnight.'
The crowd roars as two fighters clash in a blur of steel and sweat. A bookie shouts odds over the noise.
A masked combatant dominates the ring. Whispers spread: 'That's {npc_name}. Dangerous reputation.'
A brutal match ends suddenly. The victor raises their weapon as the crowd chants their name.
A newcomer enters the arena. Their stance is shaky, but the crowd loves an underdog.
A fight is interrupted when a hidden blade is discovered. Tension ripples through the stands.
A handler sizes you up. 'You look capable. Rules are simple: survive.'
The arena master nods. 'We'll find you an opponent. Try not to die — paperwork is annoying.'
A veteran fighter smirks at you. 'Hope you brought a healer.'
A hush falls as you step toward the ring. The crowd loves fresh blood.
A handler warns you: 'Some fighters cheat. Don't turn your back.'

results.success: You read the fighters well and place a smart bet. The payout nets you {amount} gp.
results.failure: You misjudge the match. Your chosen fighter falls quickly, and your coin goes with them.

results: A scarred figure approaches. 'You've got potential. Meet me near {location} after dark.'
A bookie slips you a coded note. 'If you understand this, you know where to go.'
A masked spectator whispers: 'Ask for {npc_name}. They handle… arrangements.'
A fighter offers you a job. 'Not legal. Pays well.'
A quiet observer studies you. 'You're not from around here. That's useful.'

A fighter recommends you to a local gang. 'They need muscle for a risky job.'
A patron offers coin for a private duel. 'Discreet, of course.'

A mercenary captain is recruiting for a job outside the city. Won't say what, but the pay is {amount} gp and equipment is provided.

A local lord needs a small group to clear a location that can't be identified officially. Very good pay. Very unofficial.
Bounty work: a dangerous individual was last seen near {location}. Wanted alive. {amount} gp.
A note pinned to the board: 'Soldiers of fortune wanted for a dangerous operation. Experience with {creature} preferred.'
'The city guard won't handle it,' a civilian explains. 'Too political. I need someone who doesn't care about politics.'
A job nobody else will take is still available. The pay reflects that: {amount} gp up front, more on completion.
A guild is hiring outside contractors for something they can't assign to their own members. Deniability is the point.
'People talk here,' the madam says. 'Someone saw {npc_name} sneaking into the {district} last night.'
A worker whispers: 'There's tension brewing between two guilds. Something big is coming.'
A patron mutters about a secret meeting held near {location}. 'High-ranking folks.'
A performer mentions strange visitors asking about a lost {item}.
'Rumors?' someone laughs. 'This place runs on them. But some are dangerous to repeat.'
A well-dressed patron introduces themselves discreetly. 'I deal in information. For the right price.'
A performer recognizes you. 'You helped {npc_name}, didn't you? I owe them a favor.'
The madam nods toward a private booth. 'Someone wants to speak with you.'
A quiet worker slips you a folded note. 'From a friend. Don't read it here.'
A patron offers assistance. 'I know people in the {district}. Useful people.'
The madam provides a detailed account of recent events involving {npc_name}.
A performer shares what they overheard: a plot unfolding near {location}.
A worker reveals a secret passage used by smugglers beneath the {district}.
A patron quietly warns you about a dangerous {creature} sighted nearby.
Today's lots include several interesting items. The bidding on one becomes unexpectedly intense — two buyers clearly want it beyond its stated value.
A lot is pulled from auction mid-bidding. No explanation given. The auctioneer moves on immediately. Several observers seem unsurprised.
An item catches your eye and you start bidding. You win a bid on an item for {amount} gp. As it's handed over, you notice something about it that wasn't mentioned in the description.
A {item} appears among the lots—someone in the room visibly tenses up when it's announced. At first glance, it looks like a {npc_role}
An item appears that shouldn't be in public circulation. It sells quickly, to a buyer who clearly came prepared. Use Oracle: what was it and who bought it?

results.success: The auction house accepts your item for consignment. Commission: 15%. Estimated sale date: within two weeks. They will send payment to wherever you specify.
results.failure: The appraiser declines your item. 'The current market for this isn't strong. We'd struggle to move it at a price worth your time or ours.'

The upcoming lots are standard — until one entry near the bottom: an item whose description is deliberately vague.
'We have a {item} coming in from a property in the {district},' says the clerk. 'The previous owner seemed eager to get rid of it.'
A medic hands you a neatly wrapped kit. 'Basic but reliable,' they say with a tired smile.
You find a shelf of freshly prepared poultices. The scent of herbs fills the air.
A nurse retrieves a potion from a locked cabinet. 'Use sparingly — we're low on stock.'
A shipment of medical supplies arrives from {location}. The staff rushes to unpack it.
A doctor offers you a discounted healing potion. 'Leftover from yesterday's batch — still effective.'

Receive treatment.confirmation: Pay 20 gp for full medical treatment?
results: The healers work methodically and without ceremony. You leave in significantly better condition than you arrived.
Treatment is thorough and professional. The healer notes your injuries with clinical interest before treating them.
A combination of mundane medicine and minor healing magic. Effective regardless of the method.
The healer treats your injuries and adds: 'Whatever did this — be more careful next time. Or don't come back. We're getting busy.'

results.success: A healer speaks carefully: 'We've had several admissions this week from near {location}. Same type of injuries. Not from fights — from something else. We reported it to the guard. I don't think they followed up.'
results.failure: The staff is discreet about patient information. 'We don't share that. Privacy of care.' They're politely but firmly unhelpful.

results: Shelves packed with gear greet you. The shopkeeper says, 'If you can't find it here, you probably don't need it.'
A crate of new stock sits open. Inside are tools stamped with the mark of {location}.
You find a curious trinket tucked behind a row of lanterns. The shopkeeper shrugs. 'No idea how that got there.'
A rack of travel rations smells surprisingly fresh. 'Baked this morning,' the shopkeeper boasts.
A display of tools catches your eye. One of them seems oddly well-crafted for such a mundane item.
The shopkeeper inspects your goods. 'Fair quality. I can offer {amount}.'
'Where did you get this?' they ask, intrigued. The offer rises slightly after your explanation.
The shopkeeper declines politely. 'Too much stock already. Try the Central Market.'
A customer overhears and immediately expresses interest. The shopkeeper mediates a quick sale.
The shopkeeper offers a lower price than expected, citing slow demand. Their reasoning is annoyingly sound.
'Rumors?' the shopkeeper says. 'Plenty. Someone saw strange tracks near {location}.'
A customer whispers: 'They say {npc_name} has been acting strangely. Avoid their shop.'
The shopkeeper leans in. 'A caravan went missing last week. No survivors found.'
'Something's off in the {district},' the shopkeeper mutters. 'People are nervous.'
A traveler mentions a hidden cache of goods buried outside town. 'If you believe such tales.'
'Specialty items?' the shopkeeper says. 'I can order them — delivery in 1d4 days.'
They check a ledger. 'Out of stock, but {npc_name} might have one.'
'I can get that,' the shopkeeper says, 'but it'll cost extra. Rare goods attract attention.'
The shopkeeper hesitates. 'I had one last month. Sold it to someone from {location}.'
'Special orders require a deposit,' they say. 'No exceptions.'
The clerk counts your coin with meticulous precision. 'Your deposit is secure. Withdraw anytime during daylight hours.'
A stern banker records your deposit in a heavy ledger. 'We guarantee full protection,' they say, tapping the page.
A junior clerk fumbles with the lockbox before securing your gold. 'Sorry — first week on the job.'
The banker nods approvingly. 'Wise choice. Streets near {location} have been unsafe lately.'
A guard escorts your deposit to the vault. You hear the heavy door seal shut behind them.

results.success: Loan approved: up to {amount} gp at 10% interest per month. Collateral required. The clerk is professional and efficient. The paperwork is thorough.
results.failure: Loan declined. 'Insufficient collateral or credit history.' The clerk suggests returning with a guarantor or proof of regular income.

results: The appraiser examines each piece carefully and offers 90% of assessed value — better than most alternatives in the city.
A straightforward transaction. The bank pays fair market value and asks no questions about provenance.
The appraiser pauses at one piece. 'This is unusual. I'd like to have it looked at before we finalize the price. Two hours.'
Quick transaction. Clean price. The banker adds: 'If you come across more of this quality, we buy regularly.'

results.success: The banker speaks carefully, maintaining plausible professional discretion. 'I can't name clients. But I can tell you that someone with significant resources has been making large withdrawals recently. Unusual pattern.'
results.failure: The banker is politely impenetrable. Client confidentiality is absolute. They suggest speaking to the merchant guild if you need introductions to investors.

results.success: The smith listens to your specifications, asks two clarifying questions, and names a price: {amount} gp, five to ten days depending on complexity. 'I do good work. It takes the time it takes.'
results.failure: The smith is backlogged. 'Three weeks minimum before I can start. Come back then, or take what's on the shelf.'

Repair damaged equipment.confirmation: Pay 10 gp to repair your damaged equipment?
results: Quick fix: the damage wasn't as bad as it looked. The blacksmith is charging less than expected.
The blacksmith examines the damage. 'This mark… I could be wrong, but it looks like the bite of a {creature}. 'I've heard they roam east of the city.
'Strange… I've seen this exact type of fracture before.' The blacksmith mentions another customer from the {district} who brought in something similar.

The blacksmith pauses. 'I can repair it, but if you ever want to reinforce it, bring me a fragment of {item}. With that, I could create something unique. But I warn you, it's a long and expensive project.'

The hammer strikes the glowing iron and sparks dance in a pattern that doesn't seem random. The blacksmith looks up, wiping his forehead with the back of his hand: 'See? They chase each other like spirits searching for a home. They remind me of certain tales that come from {location}. You've never been there, have you?'

The repair takes longer than expected, and in the meantime, the blacksmith starts talking to you. 'Listen,' the blacksmith says in a low voice, 'if you happen to encounter a {creature}, let {npc_name}, the {npc_role}, know. They've been searching for signs of it for days, and honestly, I can't stand hearing him ramble on about it anymore.'

The gunsmith examines your weapon. 'This wear pattern is unusual—it looks like you fought a {creature}. I might be wrong, but I'm asking because {npc_name} says they spotted some outside the city a few days ago.'
A {npc_role} is waiting in line behind you; the number of weapons they're carrying is embarrassing.
'That special order?' the gunsmith lowers his voice. 'Someone paid {amount} gp to have everything ready by tomorrow. That never happens.'
A handler presents a sturdy mount. 'Reliable temperament,' they say. The animal snorts softly, as if agreeing.
You notice a sleek creature with bright eyes. 'Fast, but spirited,' the handler warns. 'Not for the faint of heart.'
A merchant leads out a mount with unusual markings. 'Came from near {location}. Hardy stock.'
A young stablehand offers you a discount on a mount that seems overly affectionate. It nudges your shoulder insistently.
A handler hesitates. 'This one… chooses its rider.' The mount studies you with unsettling intelligence.
The handler inspects your animal carefully. 'Healthy. I can offer {amount}.'
'Where did you get this one?' the merchant asks, impressed. Their offer rises slightly.
The handler declines politely. 'Too old for resale. But I can direct you to someone who might take it.'
A buyer overhears and immediately expresses interest, offering a competitive price.
The merchant offers a lower price than expected, citing minor issues. Their reasoning is annoyingly sound.
You browse a selection of well-crafted saddles. One is engraved with runes of unknown origin.
A handler recommends a durable harness. 'Used by caravan guards. Won't fail you.'
You find a set of reins woven with colorful thread. 'Traditional craft from {location},' the merchant explains.
A crate of new gear arrives. The merchant mutters: 'Finally. Been waiting on this shipment.'
A stablehand offers you a discount on slightly worn tack. 'Still good,' they insist.
'Exotic?' the handler says. 'Try the outskirts near {location}. Traders pass through with strange beasts.'
The merchant lowers their voice. 'Someone came asking the same thing. They mentioned {npc_name}.'
